QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_35

question.text: In case of death of an NPS subscriber under the All Citizens Model, what happens to the corpus?

answer.text: 40% annuity and 60% lump sum to nominee
80% annuity and 20% lump sum to nominee
Entire corpus paid as lump sum to nominee
Corpus is forfeited to the government

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On death of a subscriber under the All Citizens Model, the entire accumulated corpus is paid as a lump sum to the nominee. **The Trap:** Under the Government Model, death benefits are different: 80% annuity and 20% lump sum (or full lump sum if Rs.5 lakh or less). **Cross-Exam Context:** Death benefits differ by model: All Citizens = entire lump sum to nominee. Govt Model = 80% annuity + 20% lump sum. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure clients have updated nominations. The death benefit structure makes nomination critical for smooth claim settlement.
